articles.title: Back-To-Back Powerful Earthquakes Hit Venezuela, Causing Widespread Damage
articles.content:
加拉加斯，委内瑞拉（美联社）——委内瑞拉代理总统表示，星期三傍晚连续发生的两次强烈地震造成至少32人死亡、至少700人受伤。随着救援人员在倒塌建筑中搜救、急救人员抵达受灾地区，死亡人数预计还将上升。这两次地震分别为7.2级和7.5级，发生在下午6点刚过不久。

代理总统德尔西·罗德里格斯在星期三晚间向全国发表的讲话中宣布国家进入紧急状态，并表示地震在多个州造成损害。星期四早些时候公布的伤亡数字不包括拉瓜伊拉州，罗德里格斯称该州为“灾难区”，是受灾最严重的地区。

她在该州表示：“那里有数十栋建筑倒塌，位于加拉加斯以北约30公里（19英里），我们目前正在开展密集救援行动以挽救生命。”

这两次地震是委内瑞拉一个多世纪以来遭遇的最强烈地震之一，影响范围波及整个地区，建筑物在多个城市被疏散，距离委内瑞拉首都加拉加斯约1700公里（1050英里）的巴西亚马逊地区也有震感。

委内瑞拉国家电视台VTV星期四早些时候播放的画面显示，在重灾区拉瓜伊拉，三名满身灰尘但幸存的儿童从废墟中被救出。该电视台还报道称，位于加拉加斯西北约200公里（120英里）的图卡卡斯市一家医院受损，画面显示数十名穿着类似医疗服装的人员站在建筑前面。

罗德里格斯表示，地震损坏了加拉加斯附近的主要机场——西蒙·玻利瓦尔国际机场并导致其关闭。她还表示，加拉加斯的地铁和天然气服务已被取消。她敦促委内瑞拉人通过政府应用程序报告任何损害情况。

罗德里格斯表示，学校课程将暂停数天。教育部表示，部分校舍将被用作避难所和捐赠中心。

“我们敦促民众保持冷静，”罗德里格斯说，并请求医护人员前往医院协助救治伤员。“我们敦促团结。”

该国两次遭遇强震

美国地质调查局最初表示，第一次地震震级为7.1级，后修正为7.2级。震中位于该国加勒比海岸莫龙镇以西约168公里（104英里）处，距离加拉加斯西部。震源深度为22公里（13.6英里）。

美国地质调查局报告称，仅一分钟后发生了更大的7.5级地震。第二次地震震源深度为10公里（6.2英里），震中位于莫龙西南约16公里（10英里）处。

在沿海的法尔孔州，州长维克托·克拉克表示，地震发生后数小时内已有32人住院，15人被困。

国际援助伸出援手

包括美国、阿根廷、玻利维亚、巴西、智利、厄瓜多尔、萨尔瓦多、墨西哥、巴拿马和乌拉圭在内的多个国家政府提供了援助。

美国国务卿鲁比奥星期四早些时候在X上发帖称，美国正在“立即向委内瑞拉部署搜救队、医疗资源和人道主义援助”。

美国负责外国援助的副国务卿杰里米·P·列文表示，美国国务院已动员了一个灾害援助小组和特别工作组，与委内瑞拉临时政府协调援助工作。

罗德里格斯星期四表示，卡塔尔已经派出救援人员，预计次日抵达委内瑞拉，墨西哥和萨尔瓦多的救援人员也将到来。

曾与委内瑞拉政府立场截然相反的萨尔瓦多总统纳伊布·布克尔星期三晚间在X上发帖表示已提供援助。

布克尔写道：“我们向你们致以全部的声援和祈祷。坚持住，委内瑞拉。”

厄瓜多尔总统丹尼尔·诺博亚表示，他已下令立即运送人道主义援助以应对紧急情况。

诺博亚写道：“厄瓜多尔将以这个时刻所需要的速度和承诺作出回应，因为尽管我们存在巨大分歧，人道主义必须始终引领领导者的行动。”

玻利维亚总统罗德里戈·帕斯表示，他的国家随时准备提供任何需要的援助。就在不到一周前，由于持续数周的反政府抗议活动，玻利维亚刚刚宣布进入紧急状态。

巴西总统路易斯·伊纳西奥·达席尔瓦的政府表达了声援，并表示没有巴西人报告受伤。

“我们都不得不离开家”

星期四的电视广播显示了救援人员在倒塌建筑上使用电动工具的画面。

地震发生时，加拉加斯的人们从摇晃的建筑物中疏散出来，许多人看到倒塌的墙壁后明显受到惊吓，家具从街道上就能看到。两个通常是繁忙餐厅和其他商业场所的首都社区可以看到灰尘柱。

人们在街上待了好几个小时，一些人坐在地上抱着宠物，灰尘在他们周围聚集。倒塌的建筑、倒下的电线杆和碎片阻塞了街道。首都部分地区停电，手机信号中断。

加拉加斯居民埃克托尔·里奇说：“开始很轻微，然后逐渐增强，最后我们都不得不离开家，出去聚在一起。”

另一位加拉加斯居民罗伯托·加马斯说，他所在的建筑“真的左右剧烈摇晃。太不真实了。力量非常强大。”

委内瑞拉部分地区手机信号中断，加深了许多家庭的焦虑，特别是在该国持续危机期间已经离开的超过770万人中。

流亡海外、去年12月离开委内瑞拉的委内瑞拉反对派领导人玛丽亚·科里娜·马查多在X上发帖祈祷并祝愿委内瑞拉人坚强。

她在X上写道：“愿在这个困难时刻，我们之间能充满力量、平静和团结。”

影响遍及委内瑞拉全境

委内瑞拉内政部长迪奥斯达多·卡韦略表示，地震在多个州都有震感，并要求驾车者为救护车和其他紧急车辆让路。

卡韦略说：“我们理解一些人可能很绝望，但我们正在按照规程行动，激活援助和救援工作以帮助最需要帮助的人。”他敦促人们待在室外，因为余震可能进一步损坏建筑物。“照顾好儿童和老人。互相打电话，确认没有人受伤。”

地震影响整个地区

据TV Globo报道，巴西亚马逊地区的马瑙斯、贝伦和马卡帕建筑物被疏散。地震在哥伦比亚加勒比海和东北地区也有震感，但没有损坏或人员伤亡的报告。

美国太平洋海啸预警中心在地震后发布了多项海啸警报，但很快被解除。

委内瑞拉发生强烈地震的情况并不常见。虽然该国位于多条断层线附近，但其位于南美洲板块和加勒比板块的交界处，使得地震比拉丁美洲其他地区少得多。

地震在太平洋沿岸地区频繁发生，包括墨西哥和智利，这两个国家都位于被称为“火环”的地震活跃构造带上。美国地质调查局表示，该地区占全球地震的90%。

——加西亚·卡诺于哥伦比亚波哥大报道。美联社记者克拉拉·普雷韦于布宜诺斯艾利斯、阿斯特丽德·苏亚雷斯于哥伦比亚波哥大、毛里西奥·萨瓦雷斯于圣保罗、印度-凯瑟琳·布里吉达、梅根·贾内茨基和印度·格兰特于墨西哥城、克里斯蒂娜·富恩特斯于马德里以及玛丽亚·特雷莎·埃尔南德斯于北京对此报道亦有贡献。
articles.summary:
CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Powerful back-to-back earthquakes that struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening killed at least 32 people and injured at least 700, the nation’s acting president said, as communities across the South American country sustained damage.

Acting President Delcy Rodríguez warned the toll was expected to rise as rescuers search collapsed buildings and emergency crews reach devastated areas after the 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes that struck shortly after 6 p.m.

Advertisement

Rodríguez declared a state of emergency in an address to the nation late Wednesday and said the quakes caused damage in several states. The casualty figures released early Thursday excluded the state of La Guaira, which Rodríguez described as a “disaster zone” and the area hardest hit.

“Dozens of buildings have collapsed there, about 30 kilometers (19 miles) north of Caracas, and we are currently carrying out intensive rescue operations to save lives,” she said.

The earthquakes, among the strongest to strike Venezuela in more than a century, roiled the region, with buildings evacuated in cities and areas impacted as far as Brazil’s Amazon about 1,700 kilometers (1,050 miles) from Venezuela’s capital Caracas.

Advertisement

Venezuela’s state-run VTV showed footage early Thursday of three children, covered in dust but alive, being pulled from the rubble in hard-hit La Guaira. The broadcaster also said a hospital in the city of Tucacas, about 200 kilometers (120 miles) northwest of Caracas, suffered damage and showed images of dozens of people in what seemed like medical garb in front of the building.

The earthquakes damaged and closed Simón Bolívar International Airport near Caracas, the country’s main airport, Rodríguez said, adding that subway and natural gas services in Caracas were canceled. She urged Venezuelans to report any damages through a government app.

Rodríguez said school classes would be canceled for several days. The Ministry of Education said some school buildings would be used as shelters and donation centers.

Advertisement

“We urge our population to remain calm,” said Rodríguez, who asked health care professionals to report to hospitals to assist the injured. “We urge unity.”

Country Hit Twice By Large Quakes

The U.S. Geological Survey initially said the first earthquake had a magnitude of 7.1, later revising that to 7.2. Its epicenter was west of Morón on the country’s Caribbean coast about 168 kilometers (104 miles) west of Caracas. The quake had a depth of 22 kilometers (13.6 miles).

Advertisement

The USGS reported an even larger 7.5-magnitude earthquake just a minute later. The second quake had a depth of 10 kilometers (6.2 miles) with an epicenter 16 kilometers (10 miles) southwest of Morón.

In the coastal state of Falcon, Gov. Víctor Clark said 32 people had been hospitalized and there were 15 people trapped in the hours after the earthquake.

Advertisement

International Assistance Offered

Offers of help were made by various governments including the United States, Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Ecuador, El Salvador, Mexico, Panama and Uruguay.

U.S. Secretary of State Rubio said in a post on X early Thursday that the United States is “immediately deploying search and rescue teams, medical resources, and humanitarian assistance to Venezuela.”

Jeremy P. Lewin, the U.S. undersecretary of state for foreign assistance, said the State Department had mobilized a disaster assistance team and task force to coordinate aid in coordination with the interim Venezuelan government.

Advertisement

Rodríguez said Thursday that Qatar had already sent rescuers who were expected in Venezuela the next day, along with rescue personnel from Mexico and El Salvador.

El Salvador President Nayib Bukele, once diametrically opposed to Venezuela’s government, said in a post on X Wednesday night that he had offered aid.

“We send you all our solidarity and our prayers. Stay strong, Venezuela,” Bukele wrote.

Ecuadorian President Daniel Noboa said he had ordered the immediate delivery of humanitarian aid to help respond to the emergency.

Advertisement

“Ecuador will respond with the speed and commitment this moment demands because, despite our enormous differences, humanity must always guide the actions of a leader,” Noboa wrote.

Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz, who less than a week ago declared a state of emergency in his country following weeks of anti-government protests, said his country stood ready to provide any needed assistance.

The administration of Brazil President Luiz Inácio da Silva expressed solidarity and said no Brazilians reported being injured.

Advertisement

‘We All Had To Leave Our Houses’

Television broadcasts Thursday showed images of rescue workers using power tools on collapsed structures.

During the quakes, people evacuated swaying buildings in Caracas, many visibly shocked as they saw collapsed walls that left furniture visible from the street. Dust columns could be seen in two capital neighborhoods with typically busy restaurants and other businesses.

Advertisement

People remained on the streets for hours, some sitting on the ground hugging pets as dust gathered around them. Collapsed buildings, toppled electric poles and debris blocked streets. Parts of the capital lost power and cellphone signal.

“It started off gently and then gradually grew, and in the end, we all had to leave our houses, go outside and gather together,” Caracas resident Hector Ricci said.

Roberto Gamas, another Caracas resident, said the building he was in “really shook from side to side. Unreal. The force was incredibly strong.”

Advertisement

The lack of cellphone signal in parts of Venezuela deepened the distress of many families, particularly those among the more than 7.7 million people who have left the country during its protracted crisis.

Venezuela opposition leader María Corina Machado, in exile after leaving Venezuela in December, took to X to send prayers and wish strength to Venezuelans.

“May strength, serenity, and solidarity prevail among us in the face of this difficult time,” she said on X.

Advertisement

Impact Felt Throughout Venezuela

Venezuela Interior Minister Diosdado Cabello said the quake could be felt in several states and asked motorists to give way to ambulances and other emergency vehicles.

“We understand that some people may be desperate, but we are acting according to protocols to activate aid and rescue efforts to help those who need it most,” Cabello said, urging people to remain outside as aftershocks could further damage structures. “Be very careful with children and the elderly. Call each other and check that no one has been harmed.”

Advertisement

Earthquakes Impact The Region

Buildings in Manaus, Belem and Macapá in Brazil’s Amazon were evacuated, according to reports on TV Globo. The quakes also were felt in Colombia’s Caribbean and northeast regions, but there were no reports of damages or injuries.

The U.S. Pacific Tsunami Warning Center issued several tsumani alerts in the wake of the earthquakes that were quickly lifted.

Strong earthquakes are unusual in Venezuela. While the country sits near multiple fault lines, its position straddling the South American and Caribbean plates make earthquakes much less common than in other parts of Latin America.

Advertisement

Earthquakes are frequent along the Pacific coast, including in Mexico and Chile, which both sit along the seismically active tectonic belt known as the Ring of Fire, an area that the USGS said is responsible for 90% of earthquakes.

___

Garcia Cano reported from Bogota, Colombia. Associated Press writers Clara Preve in Buenos Aires, Astrid Suarez in Bogota, Colombia, Mauricio Savarese in Sao Paulo and Anna-Catherine Brigida, Megan Janetsky and India Grant in Mexico City, Cristina Fuentes in Madrid and Maria Teresa Hernandez in Beijing contributed to this report.

Related
VenezuelaEarthquakesvenezuela earthquake 2026

articles.title: Dispute Over Nuclear Inspections Shows How U.S. And Iran Are Negotiating In Public
articles.content:
东京（美联社）——联合国核机构负责人周三表示，作为美国与伊朗达成的临时协议的一部分，他的检查员将访问伊朗铀浓缩设施。而伊朗一名外交官则坚持认为，任何此类访问只能在最终协议达成后进行。

这些言论呼应了前一天美国和伊朗在核检查问题上相互矛盾的表态。自两国签署协议以来的一周内，双方领导人多次公开对这份文件的实际含义持不同意见。

国际原子能机构负责人拉斐尔·马里亚诺·格罗西周三承认了围绕伊朗核计划的“口水战”。但双方的说法在多个战线展开博弈，包括以色列与伊朗支持的黎巴嫩真主党武装分子的战争，以及德黑兰将如何支配一旦解冻的数十亿美元资金。

通过签署谅解备忘录，美国和伊朗同意用60天时间来敲定这些及其他细节。在此期间——私下谈判的同时——两国领导人也将继续公开协商，这增加了该地区脆弱停火协议脱轨的风险。

以色列与真主党之间的战斗周三再度爆发，这对美伊外交构成威胁。以色列发动空袭，造成黎巴嫩南部两人死亡，该国国有通讯社报道称。这是周六最新停火生效以来以色列对黎巴嫩的首次空袭。以色列军方暂未对此次袭击置评。

联合国核机构负责人称检查将会进行

自2025年以色列对伊朗发动为期12天的战争以来，国际原子能机构一直被德黑兰阻止访问铀浓缩设施。外界认为伊朗储存了足够的高浓缩铀，在选择的情况下可制造多达10枚核武器。伊朗坚称其计划是和平的，尽管伊朗是世界上唯一一个在无武器计划情况下将铀浓缩至60%纯度的国家。

格罗西的言论是联合国这家机构迄今为止最明确的表态，该机构在确定伊朗核储备状况方面至关重要。

“我能理解政治声明，它们是现实的一部分，但我想提醒你们并提请你们注意的基本事实是，有一份由两国总统签署的谅解备忘录，”他在遭受海啸袭击的福岛第一核电站表示。

该协议“明确指出，关于核材料设施的核活动将接受国际原子能机构的监督——一字不差，”他说。

“显然，要做到这一点，我们必须进行检查，”格罗西说。“无论这发生在后天、一周后还是十天后，这很重要，但不是必需的。这将会发生。”

该协议要求伊朗的高浓缩铀进行“降级处理”。

伊朗副外长卡泽姆·加里巴巴迪在格罗西讲话后予以反击，称他在瑞士期间未与格罗西会面。

“这些问题将仅在最终协议的框架内，并根据对方实际取消所有制裁和其他措施的举措来审查和决定。”加里巴巴迪在X平台上写道。

他补充道：“你不能用媒体炒作来推进‘煽动然后接管’的政策。”

国际原子能机构被阻止访问被炸地点

自2025年战争以来，国际原子能机构已被允许访问伊朗其他核设施。但国际原子能机构表示，如果不进入铀浓缩设施，就无法核实伊朗核储备的状况。伊朗和国际原子能机构均表示，德黑兰尚未进行铀浓缩，但防扩散专家担心伊斯兰共和国可能正在转移其核储备。

但脆弱的停火协议已经面临考验，伊朗表示，由于以色列与伊朗支持的黎巴嫩真主党武装之间的战斗，伊朗再次关闭了霍尔木兹海峡。

以色列国防部长周三表示，美国并未要求以色列从黎巴嫩撤军。以色列领导人本雅明·内塔尼亚胡随后宣布：“只要我还是总理，我们将维护黎巴嫩南部的安全区。”

黎巴嫩和以色列官员本周在华盛顿举行会谈，作为两国直接谈判的一部分，黎巴嫩希望通过谈判达成以色列撤军计划。

巴基斯坦外交部周三表示，美国和伊朗之间的技术级会谈预计将于下周初在瑞士恢复。巴基斯坦一直是关键调解方。

美国有计划监督伊朗冻结资金

临时协议还包括解冻伊朗数十亿美元资产的承诺。美国总统唐纳德·特朗普希望这笔钱用于购买美国种植的农作物，但伊朗官员表示，他们应该决定资金的使用方式。

美国财政部长斯科特·贝森特表示，他的部门将在卡塔尔派驻人员监督资金的流向。他在接受CNBC采访时表示，伊朗将把“很大一部分”解冻资金用于购买“美国食品和药品”。

“我们将把钱循环回美国产品，”贝森特说。

马可·鲁比奥正在中东

美国国务卿马可·鲁比奥在波斯湾进行三国访问，美国国务院周三表示，他首先在阿布扎比与阿联酋总统谢赫·穆罕默德·本·扎耶德·阿勒纳哈扬举行会晤。

“我们不会做任何损害盟友安全的事情，”鲁比奥后来在科威特表示。特朗普政府在那里宣布有限重新开放在伊朗战争最激烈时期关闭的美国大使馆。

在前往巴林之前，鲁比奥表示，正在进行的谈判包括创建“数百个具体领域”，黎巴嫩军队可以在这些地方确保其领土安全。他称这些讨论是进程的一部分，并表示这不会“一夜之间”发生。

——甘布尔尔发自阿联酋迪拜。美联社记者朱莉娅·弗兰克尔在耶路撒冷、马修·李在阿布扎比、穆尼尔·艾哈迈德在伊斯兰堡对此报道亦有贡献。

TOKYO (AP) — The head of the U.N.’s nuclear agency said Wednesday that Iranian nuclear enrichment sites would be visited by his inspectors as part of the interim U.S.-Iran deal to reach an end to the war. An Iranian diplomat instead insisted any such visit would only come after a final deal.

The comments echoed contradictory remarks about nuclear inspections a day earlier from the U.S. and Iran. During the week since the two countries signed the deal, their leaders have repeatedly disagreed in public about what that document actually means.

Advertisement

International Atomic Energy Agency head Rafael Mariano Grossi on Wednesday acknowledged the “war of words” over Iran’s nuclear program. But the dueling narratives are playing out on several fronts, including Israel’s war with Iranian-backed Hezbollah militants in Lebanon and how Tehran will spend billions of dollars once unfrozen.

Through the signing of the memorandum of understanding, the U.S. and Iran agreed to a 60-day period to iron out these and other details. Until that happens — during private talks — leaders from both countries will also continue to negotiate in public, raising the risks of derailing the shaky ceasefire in the region.

The fighting between Israel and Hezbollah, a threat to the U.S.-Iran diplomacy, flared on Wednesday. Israel launched an airstrike that killed two people in southern Lebanon, the country’s state-run news agency said. It was Israel’s first airstrike on Lebanon since the latest ceasefire took effect on Saturday. There was no immediate comment from the Israeli military on the strike.

Advertisement

UN’s Nuclear Agency Head Says Inspections Will Happen

Since Israel launched a 12-day war on Iran in 2025, the IAEA has been blocked by Tehran from visiting enrichment sites. The Islamic Republic is believed to store enough highly enriched uranium to potentially build as many as 10 nuclear weapons, should it choose. Iran maintains that its program is peaceful, though it is the only country in the world to have uranium enriched up to 60% purity without a weapons program.

Grossi’s remarks were the firmest yet from the United Nations agency, which is central to determining the status of Iran’s nuclear stockpile.

Advertisement

“I can understand political statements, they are part of the reality, but the fundamental thing I would like to remind you and draw your attention to is that there has been a memorandum of understanding, signed by both presidents,” he said at the tsunami-hit Fukushima Daiichi nuclear power plant.

The accord “says explicitly that the nuclear activities that are going to be carried out with regards to the nuclear material facilities will be supervised by the IAEA — in all letters,” he said.

“Obviously, to do that, we will have to inspect,” Grossi said. “Whether this happens the day after tomorrow or in one week or in 10 days, it’s important, but not essential. This is going to happen.”

Advertisement

The deal calls for Iran’s uranium to be “downblended” from highly enriched levels.

Kazem Gharibabadi, an Iranian deputy foreign minister, took a swipe at Grossi after his remarks, saying Tehran didn’t meet with him while in Switzerland.

“These issues will be reviewed and decided only within the framework of a final agreement and as a result of practical action by the other side to end all sanctions and other measures.” Gharibabadi wrote on X.

He added: “You cannot advance the ‘stir up and take over’ policy with media hype.”

Advertisement

IAEA Blocked From Seeing Bombed Sites

The IAEA has been allowed to visit other nuclear sites in Iran since the 2025 war. But without accessing the enrichment sites, the IAEA says it can’t verify the status of Iran’s stockpile. Both Iran and the IAEA say Tehran hasn’t been enriching uranium, but nonproliferation experts worry the Islamic Republic may be moving its stockpile.

The U.S. and Iran agreed to the deal last week that calls for Tehran to dilute its stockpile of enriched uranium and waives U.S.-backed sanctions on Iranian oil.

But the uneasy ceasefire already has been tested by Iran saying it closed the Strait of Hormuz again over fighting between Israel and the Iranian-backed militia Hezbollah in Lebanon.

Advertisement

Israel’s defense minister said Wednesday the U.S. has not demanded that Israel withdraw from Lebanon. Israeli leader Benjamin Netanyahu later declared that “as long as I am Prime Minister, we will maintain the security zone in southern Lebanon.”

Lebanese and Israeli officials are meeting this week in Washington as part of direct negotiations between the two countries, through which Lebanon hopes to reach a plan for Israeli withdrawal.

Technical-level talks between the U.S. and Iran are expected to resume early next week in Switzerland, Pakistan’s Foreign Ministry said Wednesday. Pakistan has been a key mediator.

Advertisement

U.S. Has Plan To Oversee Iran’s Frozen Funds

The interim deal also includes a pledge to unfreeze billions in Iranian assets. U.S. President Donald Trump wants that money to go toward buying American-grown crops, but Iranian officials say they should decide how its spent.

U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said his department would have people in Qatar to oversee what happens with the funds. He said in a CNBC interview that Iran would spend “a very large percent” of its released money on “U.S. foodstuffs and medicines.”

“We will be recycling the money back into U.S. products,” Bessent said.

Marco Rubio Is In The Middle East

U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio traveled in the Persian Gulf for a three-nation tour, starting with a meeting in Abu Dhabi with Emirati President Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, the State Department said Wednesday.

Advertisement

“We’re not going to do anything that undermines the security of our allies,” Rubio later said while in Kuwait, where the Trump administration announced the limited reopening of the U.S. Embassy that was closed at the height of the Iran war.

Before leaving for Bahrain, Rubio said ongoing negotiations include the creation of “hundreds of specific areas” where Lebanon’s military could secure its territory. He called the discussions part of the process and said it’s not going to “happen overnight.”

___

Gambrell reported from Dubai, United Arab Emirates. Associated Press writers Julia Frankel in Jerusalem, Matthew Lee in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates, and Munir Ahmed in Islamabad contributed to this report.
